title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Bantry calls Gossington Hall and attempts to probe the recipient about whether their phone is malfunctioning. When the recipient expresses confusion, claiming the phone is fine, Mrs. Bantry claims her hearing is failing. She then calls Jane Marple to report that a secretary had gone to a public phone booth to falsely claim the hall's phone was broken, suggesting the secretary wanted to make a private call without being overheard.

Donald McNeil, a red-haired man, speaks with investigators Dermot Clogg and Crawford. McNeil denies having a motive for the murder of Heather Badekirk or the attempted murder of Marina Gregg. He admits he was at the scene and witnessed Heather Badekirk gasping for breath before she died from poison, but claims he saw nothing else. McNeil reveals he had previously gone upstairs to get drinks, during which he saw fashion photographer Margaret Bens and Lola Bristow. He notes Lola had dyed her hair a "Fiji style" rust red and was accompanied by an unknown, important-looking American man. McNeil also mentions that he was Marina Gregg's first husband, an identity previously considered insignificant.

At Scotland Yard, Inspector Clodd reviews files and telegrams before heading to the Savoy to interview Lola Bruce. Lola, dressed sumptuously, defends Marina Gregg and dismisses the poisoning attempt as a mistake. She admits to a past connection involving a man named Rob, noting that Marina had fallen madly in love with him twelve years prior while Lola was struggling with neurosis and her marriage. Lola explains she married Eddie Groves following her divorce from Rob to maintain stability for her children.
